commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional reconnect-delay delegate to `GatewayConnectionManager` and replaces fixed waits in `NodePairAutoApproveTests` with deterministic approval-completion synchronization.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows the fixed `Task.Delay(200)` waits in the node auto-approve tests and the hard-coded one-second reconnect delay; I did not run tests because this is a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No narrow automated repair is needed; maintainers should decide the validation state and move the draft PR forward or request clean required checks.

target.title: [Repo Assist] test(connection): make node auto-approve tests deterministic
target.body: ≡ƒñû *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*## WhatReplaces all `Task.Delay(200)` timing-based assertions in `NodePairAutoApproveTests` with deterministic synchronization primitives, and injects the 1 s post-approve reconnect delay so tests no longer wait on real wall-clock timers.Refs #460## Why / Root CauseThe tests had four `Task.Delay(200)` calls:| Line | Purpose ||------|---------|| 77 | Assert no approval fired when `requestId == null` || 96 | Assert no approval fired when operator scope is insufficient || 123 | Wait for the duplicate-requestId CAS guard to run and reject || 127 | Wait for the first approval to complete after releasing the gate |**Lines 77, 96, 123** are unnecessary: each of those handler paths executes synchronously (no `await` before the early return or CAS guard), so the assertion is safe the moment `FirePairingStatusChanged()` returns. The `Task.Delay` was just masking this.**Line 127** needs a real signal. A new `WaitForApprovalCompleteAsync()` method on `TrackingGatewayClient` sets a TCS after `NodePairApproveAsync` returns, giving the test a precise "done" signal without any timer.**Line 903 in `GatewayConnectionManager`** (`await Task.Delay(1000)`) lets the gateway process the approval before reconnecting the node. Tests can now inject `reconnectDelay: _ => Task.CompletedTask` to skip this entirely.## Changes- **`GatewayConnectionManager`**: add optional `Func<TimeSpan, Task>? reconnectDelay` constructor parameter (defaults to `Task.Delay`). Use it instead of the hardcoded `Task.Delay(1000)`.- **`NodePairAutoApproveTests`**:  - Pass `reconnectDelay: _ => Task.CompletedTask` in `CreateConnectedManager`.  - Remove all four `Task.Delay(200)` calls.  - Add `WaitForApprovalCompleteAsync()` to `TrackingGatewayClient` for the duplicate-request test.## Trade-offsThe injected delay is purely a test-time concern. Production callers use the `Task.Delay` default and are unaffected.## Test StatusΓ£à `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Connection.Tests/` ΓÇö **229 passed, 0 failed**  Γ£à `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/` ΓÇö 1928 passed (8 pre-existing failures unrelated to this change)  Γ£à `dotnet test tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/` ΓÇö 1137 passed (10 pre-existing failures unrelated to this change)> Generated by ≡ƒîê Repo Assist, see [workflow run](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw-windows-node/actions/runs/26426756199). [Learn more](https://github.com/githubnext/agentics/blob/main/docs/repo-assist.md).>> To install this [agentic workflow](https://github.com/githubnext/agentics/blob/97143ac59cb3a13ef2a77581f929f06719c7402a/workflows/repo-assist.md), run> ```> gh aw add githubnext/agentics/workflows/repo-assist.md@97143ac59cb3a13ef2a77581f929f06719c7402a> ```<!-- gh-aw-agentic-workflow: Repo Assist, engine: copilot, version: 1.0.40, model: claude-sonnet-4.6, id: 26426756199, workflow_id: repo-assist, run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw-windows-node/actions/runs/26426756199 --><!-- gh-aw-workflow-id: repo-assist -->
